title: Epidemic Spread (SIR Model)
description: The classic Susceptible → Infected → Recovered model. Watch herd immunity emerge and the epidemic curve in real time.

transmission_rate.label: Transmission Rate (R₀)
transmission_rate.description: How easily the disease spreads on contact

recovery_time.label: Recovery Time
recovery_time.description: Ticks until an infected person recovers

narration.text: 2 infected individuals in a population of 120. Everyone else is susceptible — no prior immunity.
Early exponential growth. Each infected person contacts multiple susceptibles. The epidemic is doubling rapidly.
The curve is steepening. This is the phase where hospitals fill up — most people infected in a short window.
Peak infection. Notice it slows not because the virus weakened, but because susceptible individuals are running out — recovered individuals block transmission chains.
The epidemic is declining. This is herd immunity in action — enough recovered individuals breaking chains of transmission to protect the remaining susceptible.
Not everyone got infected. The epidemic ended before reaching everyone because transmission chains broke. This is why vaccination works — it creates immune individuals without infection.
